title: Signedness of B operand of shift
body:
There is `techlibs/common/simlib.v` file in distribution. It contains shift module:

```
module \$shift (A, B, Y);
parameter A_SIGNED = 0;
parameter B_SIGNED = 0;
...
    if (B_SIGNED) begin:BLOCK1
        assign Y = $signed(B) < 0 ? A << -B : A >> B;
    end else begin:BLOCK2
        assign Y = A >> B;
    end

```

According to IEEE 1364-2005 5.1.12.

> The right operand is always treated as an unsigned number and has no effect on the signedness of
> the result.

Thus B should always be implicitly converted to unsigned, and following code is meaningless:

```
    if (B_SIGNED) begin:BLOCK1
        assign Y = $signed(B) < 0 ? A << -B : A >> B;

```

Or is there any special case that I miss?
code_blocks:
if (B_SIGNED) begin:BLOCK1
        assign Y = $signed(B) < 0 ? A << -B : A >> B;
